qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded recursion in Python TProtocol.skip() allows remote denial of service via deeply nested Thrift structs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unbounded recursion in Python TProtocol.skip() at lib/py/src/protocol/TProtocol.py:204-210, while the advisory specifically describes uncontrolled recursion in Node.js bindings. Although both issues are in the Apache Thrift library and share the same vulnerability class (CWE-674: Uncontrolled Recursion), they affect different language implementations with separate code paths and vulnerable code locations.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-r67j-r569-jrwp) describes uncontrolled recursion in Apache Thrift Node.js bindings (CWE-674), while the discovered qualifying finding targets unbounded recursion in Python TProtocol.skip() - same library but different language binding. The finding does not match the Node.js-specific vulnerable code path described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory summary: 'Apache Thrift Node.js bindings vulnerable to Uncontrolled Recursion'
Advisory affects: Apache Thrift npm package 'thrift' versions before 0.23.0
Finding: Python TProtocol.skip() unbounded recursion at lib/py/src/protocol/TProtocol.py:204-210
Finding targets: Python Thrift bindings, not Node.js bindings
Vulnerable code location differs: lib/py/src/protocol/TProtocol.py (Python) vs lib/nodejs (Node.js)
